title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Can I Take Loan Against Life Insurance Policy?
Loan against life insurance policy: A lifesaver during the financial crisis
Opting for personal loans may not always be the ideal option when you are in dire need of money and looking for a quick disbursal of the loan amount. Their high-interest rates and long waiting periods can be major hiccups. And, a loan against a life insurance policy can look like a better deal at this time.
Loan against any policy should be your last resort though, especially a life insurance plan, as it provides you with a better long-term coverage where your premiums are sorted, and it is kind of your safety net when your financial pool has dried up.
Also, when you have exhausted all your personal loan options and your CIBIL score restricts you for more, a loan against a life insurance policy will always come in handy at the time of this crisis.
There are several types of policies, from home to travel to health, but a life insurance policy is the safest when looking for a loan against any policy. Of course, you have to check the details with your lender for the types of insurance policies and where you stand
Can you get a loan against all life insurance plans?
According to the rules set by the IRDAI, i.e. the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India, any plans which have a guaranteed surrender value can offer loans. So, Term Plans and Unit Linked Insurance Plans not eligible to offer loans, while endowment plans, child plans and money back plans do offer loan options¹.
A loan against an insurance plan is offered against the acquired surrender value of the policy. This option is available to the policyholder in case there is a requirement for money before the end of the policy tenure. The rate of interest is determined by the company from time to time. The policyholder can choose to repay a part of the loan or full during the policy tenure. The rest of the amount would be recovered from the policyholder at the time of payout of the maturity benefit or on earlier death, the death benefit to the nominee².
Maximum loan amount against life insurance plans:
Lenders will always opt for the loan based on the surrender value of your life insurance policy and not the total sum insured. But, to get the benefit of this loan facility, you must have paid your premiums diligently for a period of at least three years. For this loan, the lenders are likely to offer up to 90% of the surrender value².
In case of your demise and unpaid premiums, the lender will have the right to deduct the sum of all the premiums planned and then offer the remaining balance to the nominee of your account.However, if the loan plus interest amount exceeds the surrender benefit at any point in time, the policy would be foreclosed and the same would be intimated to you as well.
Eligibility condition for loan against life insurance plans:
The eligibility criteria for a loan against life insurance policy are relaxed when compared to personal loans.
Your policy needs to be in-force with all premiums paid on time
Loan is available after a minimum of 3 years of continuous renewal.
The applicant needs to be minimum of 18 years and
The life insurance policy needs to have an acquired guaranteed surrender value to be eligible for a loan.
Lenders do not consider credit score as a parameter while offering loan against your life insurance policy as this is a secured loan. So, only the policy terms and conditions are considered³.
Application process for a loan against your Life Insurance Plan:
While some lenders may offer this benefit online, some will not. Lenders prefer you to do the paperwork offline and fill up all the forms in person when applying for such a loan. Fulfilling the formalities offline is always advisable so you don’t have to go knocking at your lender’s doors in case the online attempt doesn’t go through.When you apply for a loan against your life insurance policy, there is a conditional assignment that is done at the time of disbursal of the loan, such that if the amount is not repaid on time, the insurer can surrender the plan on the policyholder’s behalf to recover its money³. However, other terms and conditions of the policy would continue as mentioned in the policy schedule. The nomination details can be altered back to the orignal one, once the loan is repaid².
Documents required for a loan against your Life Insurance Plan:
The documents that you will require to apply for a loan against your life insurance policy are:
An ID proof (an Aadhaar card is advisable).
An address proof like your electricity bill.
Your original policy document.
Premium payment receipts of the plan.
Premiums on loans against policy can be adjusted and evenly divided according to the maturity date of your policy. So, you also have the option of paying the entire loan amount in lumpsum before the maturity period and ending the cycle of loan premiums setting your investment free.
In conclusion
Personal loans are a strict no if you are struggling with your premiums and CIBIL score for a long period of time and are looking for better alternatives. A loan against a life insurance policy can put your peace at risk, but it can also be a lifesaver if you keep each requirement in check. Usually a loan against your life insurance policy would be more economic as it is a secured loan and thus would have lower rate of interest than otherwise.
So, if you do not want to risk it much and are looking for a better loan facility, go for a loan against the life insurance policy. But plan it better in advance, so your policy does not lapse.
